post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 152: To Create a World
Credits to Binomial-theorem/chaotic_iak/willwang123 (among others) for the rule template.

Please read all the rules, there are several differences from other newbie games!

Story
[intended to be the first game of a series i'm making]

One of the least-understood phenomenons is the creation of a new world. It is unknown as to how the world is actually created - it may come about through excess energy of another, or perhaps it is created by the same forces that govern our own. Whatever the case, the world is devoid of anything until the Sentients arrive.

From our understanding, Sentients are beings which have ascended (however that works) from their own world, to arrive at the new one. They all harness immense power, and some may have more than others. They work to create a world in their own image, compromising with the other Sentients along the way. But there is one type of Sentient that others will never cooperate with - the demons.

Demons seem to pop up in every world, at some point or another. They have been described as some sort of plague on worlds. It is known that they all share a common set of worlds, which they completely control, and their primary goal is to expand their territory. If they enter a world from the very start, the world is nearly doomed.

Consistently, other Sentients have worked together to prevent these demons from overtaking worlds. But the demons seem to be growing smarter. They have mastered the art of masking as normal Sentients, and the ability to remove other Sentients from the newly formed world. With these powers, they have begun to take over all newly formed worlds. We must put a stop to them, at all costs.


Nine Sentients. That is the maximum amount that can enter a newly formed world before it becomes unstable and blocks the rest out. We can only hope it is enough.


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.


Voting/Lynches
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this; see below rule.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. So that I know you have read the rules, PM me your flavorname. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • At a Lynch-or-Lose situation, which is three town and two mafia, or two town and one mafia currently alive, votes are locked. This means that any attempt to unvote will be ignored, regardless of what the vote is.


Activity
  • Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
  • If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (summitwei) am the Moderator of Game 152. sheepsaysmeep is the co-moderator of Game 152.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. This is the comod. I have second last say


Setup
This game will use the Backup6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

Note that if applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action. The power of the Universal Backup cannot be roleblocked.

A Town Doctor is not able to self-protect.


Role PMs
Phantom (Town Tracker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Phantom (Town Tracker). Phantoms are beings (typically humans) that have died, and ascended past the underworld. You are basically invisible, and as such are able to follow people around without them noticing. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will see who that person targeted each night, if any. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
The First Sentient (Universal Backup) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are The First Sentient (Town Universal Backup). You were the first sentient to arrive at this world, and as such you hold some additional power over the others. You will inherit the ability of the first Town Power Role who dies. Your power is reflexive and cannot be roleblocked. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Twice-Ascended Sentient (Town Jailkeeper) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Twice-Ascended Sentient (Town Jailkeeper). You were a Sentient in your previous world, and took control, to the point that you could ascend again. You still have control over a small pocket world, and are able to send other Sentients to it. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will roleblock them, preventing them from using their night action, but you will also protect them. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Angel (Town Cop) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are an Angel (Town Cop). Angels are powerful beings that come from one of the least demon-ridden worlds. You have abilities that allow you to determine if someone is a normal Sentient or a demon. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will then know if they are town or antitown (or if you failed to receive results). The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Divinity (Town Doctor) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Divinity (Town Doctor). A god in your previous world, you ascended to this one, retaining most of your powers. You are able to protect one person per night from being taken by demons; however, you yourself are very weak to them, and as such you cannot protect yourself. Once a night, you may PM me a person's username. That person will then be protected from getting killed that night. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Sentient (Vanilla Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Sentient (Vanilla Townie). You ascended from your previous world for a bid to control this one, but are encountering a demon force preventing you from immediately doing so. You must take them out at all costs, before this world becomes one of theirs. Your strength comes in your voice and your vote. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Demon (Mafia Goon) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Demon (Mafia Goon). You entered this world to expand the Demonic Empire, but are encountering more trouble than expected. Your powers, however, are sure to prevail. You and your partner, [username], [role name], can return someone to their original world each night, essentially removing them from this one. During the night, you may discuss in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or nothing can prevent it.
Archdemon (Mafia Roleblocker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are an Archdemon (Mafia Roleblocker). Archdemons are the most powerful beings of the Demonic Empire, and are only sent out when the demons truly necessitate a victory. With your partner, [username], Demon (Mafia Goon), you may send someone to their original world each night, removing them from this one. In addition, your abilities as an archdemon allow you to debilitate one player per night, preventing them from doing anything for that night. During the night, you may discuss in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or nothing can prevent it.


Alive (7)
  1. +SFGiantsFan
  2. +whatshisbucket
  3. +ShineBunny
  4. jlee30 sudzoof
  5. Madelyn
  6. TeddyYT
  7. DaMightyMidget66


Dead (2)
ProGameXD, Mafia Roleblocker, was lynched Day 1. *

fishy15, Mafia Goon, was lynched Day 2. *


Significant Events
sudzoof replaces jlee30 *

Day 1 ends *
Night 1 ends *
Day 2 ends, town wins *


Warnings
ProGameXD - editing a post *


Do not post until this line is crossed out.

It is now Day 1. You may post.

The deadline for Day 1 is 12/1/17 at 4:11 PM EST.

summitwei wrote:
Story
[intended to be the first game of a series i'm making]

One of the least-understood phenomenons is the creation of a new world. It is unknown as to how the world is actually created - it may come about through excess energy of another, or perhaps it is created by the same forces that govern our own. Whatever the case, the world is devoid of anything until the Sentients arrive.

From our understanding, Sentients are beings which have ascended (however that works) from their own world, to arrive at the new one. They all harness immense power, and some may have more than others. They work to create a world in their own image, compromising with the other Sentients along the way. But there is one type of Sentient that others will never cooperate with - the demons.

Demons seem to pop up in every world, at some point or another. They have been described as some sort of plague on worlds. It is known that they all share a common set of worlds, which they completely control, and their primary goal is to expand their territory. If they enter a world from the very start, the world is nearly doomed.

Consistently, other Sentients have worked together to prevent these demons from overtaking worlds. But the demons seem to be growing smarter. They have mastered the art of masking as normal Sentients, and the ability to remove other Sentients from the newly formed world. With these powers, they have begun to take over all newly formed worlds. We must put a stop to them, at all costs.


Nine Sentients. That is the maximum amount that can enter a newly formed world before it becomes unstable and blocks the rest out. We can only hope it is enough.


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.


Voting/Lynches
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this; see below rule.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. So that I know you have read the rules, PM me your flavorname. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • At a Lynch-or-Lose situation, which is three town and two mafia, or two town and one mafia currently alive, votes are locked. This means that any attempt to unvote will be ignored, regardless of what the vote is.


Activity
  • Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
  • If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators
  • I (summitwei) am the Moderator of Game 152. sheepsaysmeep is the co-moderator of Game 152.
  • Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. This is the comod. I have second last say


Setup
This game will use the Backup6 setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

Note that if applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action. The power of the Universal Backup cannot be roleblocked.

A Town Doctor is not able to self-protect.


Role PMs
Phantom (Town Tracker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Phantom (Town Tracker). Phantoms are beings (typically humans) that have died, and ascended past the underworld. You are basically invisible, and as such are able to follow people around without them noticing. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will see who that person targeted each night, if any. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
The First Sentient (Universal Backup) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are The First Sentient (Town Universal Backup). You were the first sentient to arrive at this world, and as such you hold some additional power over the others. You will inherit the ability of the first Town Power Role who dies. Your power is reflexive and cannot be roleblocked. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Twice-Ascended Sentient (Town Jailkeeper) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Twice-Ascended Sentient (Town Jailkeeper). You were a Sentient in your previous world, and took control, to the point that you could ascend again. You still have control over a small pocket world, and are able to send other Sentients to it. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will roleblock them, preventing them from using their night action, but you will also protect them. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Angel (Town Cop) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are an Angel (Town Cop). Angels are powerful beings that come from one of the least demon-ridden worlds. You have abilities that allow you to determine if someone is a normal Sentient or a demon. Once a night, you may PM me one person's name. You will then know if they are town or antitown (or if you failed to receive results). The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Divinity (Town Doctor) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Divinity (Town Doctor). A god in your previous world, you ascended to this one, retaining most of your powers. You are able to protect one person per night from being taken by demons; however, you yourself are very weak to them, and as such you cannot protect yourself. Once a night, you may PM me a person's username. That person will then be protected from getting killed that night. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Sentient (Vanilla Townie) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Sentient (Vanilla Townie). You ascended from your previous world for a bid to control this one, but are encountering a demon force preventing you from immediately doing so. You must take them out at all costs, before this world becomes one of theirs. Your strength comes in your voice and your vote. The thread can be found here.

You win when all Mafia are killed.
Demon (Mafia Goon) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are a Demon (Mafia Goon). You entered this world to expand the Demonic Empire, but are encountering more trouble than expected. Your powers, however, are sure to prevail. You and your partner, [username], [role name], can return someone to their original world each night, essentially removing them from this one. During the night, you may discuss in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or nothing can prevent it.
Archdemon (Mafia Roleblocker) Role PM wrote:
Hello [player],


You are an Archdemon (Mafia Roleblocker). Archdemons are the most powerful beings of the Demonic Empire, and are only sent out when the demons truly necessitate a victory. With your partner, [username], Demon (Mafia Goon), you may send someone to their original world each night, removing them from this one. In addition, your abilities as an archdemon allow you to debilitate one player per night, preventing them from doing anything for that night. During the night, you may discuss in [this quicktopic]. The thread can be found here.

You win when the Mafia are the only ones left, or nothing can prevent it.
!

summitwei wrote:
Story
[intended to be the first game of a series i'm making]

One of the least-understood phenomenons is the creation of a new world. It is unknown as to how the world is actually created - it may come about through excess energy of another, or perhaps it is created by the same forces that govern our own. Whatever the case, the world is devoid of anything until the Sentients arrive.

From our understanding, Sentients are beings which have ascended (however that works) from their own world, to arrive at the new one. They all harness immense power, and some may have more than others. They work to create a world in their own image, compromising with the other Sentients along the way. But there is one type of Sentient that others will never cooperate with - the demons.

Demons seem to pop up in every world, at some point or another. They have been described as some sort of plague on worlds. It is known that they all share a common set of worlds, which they completely control, and their primary goal is to expand their territory. If they enter a world from the very start, the world is nearly doomed.

Consistently, other Sentients have worked together to prevent these demons from overtaking worlds. But the demons seem to be growing smarter. They have mastered the art of masking as normal Sentients, and the ability to remove other Sentients from the newly formed world. With these powers, they have begun to take over all newly formed worlds. We must put a stop to them, at all costs.


Nine Sentients. That is the maximum amount that can enter a newly formed world before it becomes unstable and blocks the rest out. We can only hope it is enough.


Rules
  • Read the rules. Including this one.
  • Follow all the rules. Including this one.
  • Do not post at night.
  • You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
  • Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
  • Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
  • You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others.
  • Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
  • Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
  • If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
  • Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.


Voting/Lynches
  • This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.
  • Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". For example, "Vote: fdas" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", "vote: fdas", "Vote:fdas", "Vote: fads", "Vote: fdsa", "Vote: Fdas" and "Vote: fdas" are not.
  • Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
  • Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. There is one exception to this; see below rule.
  • The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. So that I know you have read the rules, PM me your flavorname. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is.
  • You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Lynch".
  • You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
  • Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
  • The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
  • During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
  • Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
  • At a Lynch-or-Lose situation, which is three town and two mafia, or two town and one mafia currently alive, votes are locked. This means that any attempt to unvote will be ignored, regardless of what the vote is.


Activity
  • Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
  • The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
  • If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
  • Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
  • When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
  • Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
  • If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
  • If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
  • If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.


Etiquette
  • Do not use inappropriate language.
  • Do not make any personal attacks.
  • Be mature.
  • For a minor rule infraction, newbies can receive up to two warnings, SE’s up to one, and IC’s none. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled. Please do not make me modkill you.


Moderators

  • Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
  • Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
  • Please do not post in those colors or in any shades of those colors.
  • The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules. This is the comod. I have second last say

[hide=Story][intended to be the first game of a series i'm making]

One of the least-understood phenomenons is the creation of a new world. It is unknown as to how the world is actually created - it may come about through excess energy of another, or perhaps it is created by the same forces that govern our own. Whatever the case, the world is devoid of anything until the Sentients arrive.

From our understanding, Sentients are beings which have ascended (however that works) from their own world, to arrive at the new one. They all harness immense power, and some may have more than others. They work to create a world in their own image, compromising with the other Sentients along the way. But there is one type of Sentient that others will never cooperate with - the demons.

Demons seem to pop up in every world, at some point or another. They have been described as some sort of plague on worlds. It is known that they all share a common set of worlds, which they completely control, and their primary goal is to expand their territory. If they enter a world from the very start, the world is nearly doomed.

Consistently, other Sentients have worked together to prevent these demons from overtaking worlds. But the demons seem to be growing smarter. They have mastered the art of masking as normal Sentients, and the ability to remove other Sentients from the newly formed world. With these powers, they have begun to take over all newly formed worlds. We must put a stop to them, at all costs.


Nine Sentients. That is the maximum amount that can enter a newly formed world before it becomes unstable and blocks the rest out. We can only hope it is enough.
[/hide]

[hide="Rules"]
[list]
[*]Read the rules. Including this one.
[*]Follow all the rules. Including this one.
[*]Do not post at night.
[*]You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
[*]Do not quote or pretend to quote any PMs from me, including role PMs. Do not play around with exploiting this rule.
[*]Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the moderator in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so. 
[*]You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. 
[*]Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
[*]Do not pretend to edit or otherwise pretend to break a rule.
[*]If you are dead, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
[*]Do not edit the tags on the thread. This also applies if you are not playing the game.
[/list][/hide]

[hide="Voting/Lynches"]
[list]
[*][b]This game is based on deadline lynch. The player with the most votes at the time the deadline is reached is lynched. In the case of a tie, the lynch is randomly decided among the players with the most votes. Starting Day 2, majority lynch is enabled, which means day will end early if at any time a player has more than half the amount of total votes on them. If majority lynch is not reached, deadline lynch occurs.[/b]
[*]Post votes in bold font in the format "[b]Vote: <insert player here>[/b]". For example, "[b]Vote: fdas[/b]" is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: [b]fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote fdas[/b]", "[b]vote: fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote:fdas[/b]", "[b]Vote: fads[/b]", "[b]Vote: fdsa[/b]", "[b]Vote: Fdas[/b]" and "[u]Vote: fdas[/u]" are not.
[*]Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
[*]Improperly made votes (including votes in hide tags or any of the unacceptable forms above) will not be counted under any circumstances. I will not tell you if your vote is invalid. [b]There is one exception to this; see below rule.[/b]
[*]The characters "I" (capital i) and "l" (lowercase L) are considered the same character for the purpose of voting. That is, attempting to fakevote by substituting one of these characters in will not work. So that I know you have read the rules, PM me your flavorname. Most other fakevotes are acceptable, but please PM me to confirm that it is.
[*]You may vote for no lynch to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "[b]Vote: No Lynch[/b]".
[*]You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "[b]Unvote[/b]". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
[*]Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day more than half of the players currently alive are voting for a single player, that player will be lynched. It will then immediately be twilight.
[*]Starting Day 2, if at any point in a day at least half of the players alive are voting for no lynch, then there will be no lynch. It will then be twilight.
[*]The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
[*]During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
[*]Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person lynched (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.
[*]At a Lynch-or-Lose situation, which is three town and two mafia, or two town and one mafia currently alive, [b]votes are locked[/b]. This means that any attempt to unvote will be ignored, regardless of what the vote is.[/list]
[/hide]

[hide="Activity"]
[list]
[*]Each day will be 504 hours (3 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
[*]The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
[*]If the deadline is met and no person is lynched, it will be considered no lynch and will proceed to be twilight.
[*]Each night will be 3 real-life days long.
[*]When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
[*]Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night and start of the next day.
[*]If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive an in-game prod.
[*]If a player does not post in another 36-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
[*]If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless he/she posts before the moderator finds a replacement.
[*]If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in [b]bold[/b] in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long. If your V/LA will be longer than about a week, please either try to find a way to participate in the game during that time or ask for a replacement.
[/list]
[/hide]

[hide="Etiquette"]
[list]

[/hide]

[hide=Setup]This game will use the [url=https://wiki.mafiascum.net/index.php?title=Backup6]Backup6[/url] setup. This is a semi-open setup.

In this setup, there are 9 players. Three of the roles will be generated randomly by picking a row or column from the matrix. Then one Mafia Goon and 5 Vanilla Townies are added to maintain a 2:7 mafia:town ratio. The six possible setups are:
1. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Mafia Roleblocker, 1 Mafia Goon
2. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 2 Mafia Goons
3. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons
4. 6 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Jailkeeper, 2 Mafia Goons
5. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Town Cop, 1 Town Doctor, 1 Mafia Goon, 1 Mafia Roleblocker
6. 5 Vanilla Townies, 1 Universal Backup, 1 Town Tracker, 2 Mafia Goons

Note that if applicable, a Mafia Roleblocker's action takes precedence over a Town Jailkeeper's action. The power of the Universal Backup cannot be roleblocked.

A Town Doctor is [b]not[/b] able to self-protect.[/hide]

To the mortal worlds, little is known about the hierarchy of those ruled by demons. There is one Demon Lord, who rules all demon-controlled worlds, using methods unknown to others for travelling between them. After him, though, there are the archdemons.

Archdemons are the heavyweights. The big guns, brought out when a victory is really needed. In some cases, they are used to take over what could potentially be a key world in the Demonic Empire. post_rendered:
Story
Ascension is one of those taboo concepts; it is rarely spoken about except among those pursuing it. So, among the scientific community, little is known about the actual method behind it. Surely there is some secret - but it is never passed down, and either leaves or dies out with the discoverers.

But others could always follow their steps. Created with the world are several clues, encrypted in whatever way, that somehow hint towards the secret. A ritual, combined with sufficient amounts of energy, is all that is needed. This amount of energy, however, is massive, and a significant amount of would-be Sentients die trying to obtain it.


So naturally, when they ascend, nobody would want to, or even consider as a possibility, being sent back. Thankfully, nobody was.

No one died.

Story
After a world is created, it is immediately thrown up for grabs. Sentients tend to flock to these newly created worlds. In particular, the demons, looking to expand the Demonic Empire, jump to it faster than most. The average demon is created out of nothing in the fiery hells of the Demon Empire - the worlds of it are connected solely by the portals left through ascension or other such rituals. Demons born here are given one goal: Expand the Empire.

The Demon Lord is an enigmatic creature, and it is wholly unknown what he is waiting for. Surely expansion is not his endgame; but what is? Is he preparing for domination, a multiverse in which he and he alone rules? Or does he simply wish to remake the system governing even the most powerful creatures?

Whatever is the case, a current goal of expansion makes sense. The Demon Lord possesses an unexplained ability to travel between worlds. If only he could gain more of them - his travel would be limitless. It seems that he does not enjoy travelling to worlds his Empire does not control, though it is not impossible for him to do so. So, the lands that can service as portals to multiple other worlds, or service as connectors between two other demon-controlled territories, are ones that particularly warrant his attention.

This world is one of those. That is the reason why he sent two demons, one of which was one of his most powerful, over to it. The norm is one, for worlds that would be helpful but not necessary. Two is usually almost unstoppable.

But not this time. The other Sentients of this world displayed the toughest resistance the Demons have encountered. Of course, demons do not simply die; they are unique in that after being cast out they are reborn in their original realms. But it is a great setback. "We'll get them next time," they would always say, and the Lord would reply, "there won't be a next time."


On the other hand, the direction of the world had finally been realized. It finally began its construction. Some laws would have to be put in place, but that would be something to decide later. For now, this world was theirs.
